response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web developer whose primary expertise is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — not C++. His only meaningful C++ work appears to be a brief stint at National Instruments (~1 year, 2000–2001) and a bachelor thesis project, which is far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development required. He has no demonstrable capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain knowledge, and no mention of SQL Server, Oracle, or any financial platform such as Summit. While he has strong software craftsmanship fundamentals and some peripheral financial exposure via RIDE Capital, neither the technical depth in C++ nor the domain expertise required for this role is present.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture: consistent use of TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development across multiple roles
Some early C++ exposure at National Instruments building components for a professional engineering IDE
Solid communication skills evidenced by public conference talks and full-day technical workshops
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~1 year in 2000–2001) and entirely outdated; the candidate's primary stack for the past 15+ years is TypeScript/Node.js
No fixed income, fixed income derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge anywhere in the resume
No SQL Server or Oracle experience mentioned; no Windows platform development history; no Summit, Kafka, or MQ exposure
